title: DUX v9.5 Flow Object
description: Behavior orchestration defining sequences of atomic user actions - maps user workflows and journey phases. Note: Flow serves as junction object connecting Problems and Results.

object_type.description: Object type discriminator - always 'Flow' for Flow objects

end_user.description: User personas or roles who execute this flow

user_scenario.description: Context or situation that triggers this flow

id.description: The unique identifier for the Behavior object.

reference_context.description: Explanation of this behavior's role in the flow (e.g., 'key_behavior: primary user action for achieving outcome').

behavior_sequence.description: Ordered sequence of behavior IDs that make up this flow. The context can specify if a behavior is a 'key_behavior'. PROMPT GUIDANCE: When generating flows, LLM should infer and create synthetic Task-type behaviors if they don't exist. Only reference Task behaviors, not Actions.

protocol_url.description: URL to protocol, methodology, or workflow documentation for this flow

reference_context.description: LLM-generated explanation of why this relationship exists

problem_ids.description: Array of Problem objects that this flow addresses

useroutcome_id.description: ID of the UserOutcome that this flow contributes to achieving. 1:1 relationship - each flow supports one specific outcome.

evidence_maturity.description: Progressive evidence maturity tier: 01_assumptive (inferred, no direct support), 02_anecdotal (1-2 qualitative signals), 03_early_signal (recurring signal, no quant), 04_balanced_signal (qualitative + quantitative), 05_triangulated (cross-method validation)

evidence.description: Array of Provenance object IDs that support this flow's sequence and importance

tags.description: System-derived tags generated by NotebookLM DUX scan - not manually set
